title: Add agent/redaction.py â€” single chokepoint for outward-facing error strings
blocks: T-104 (Telegram + god log wiring needs safe_error)
current_state: Exception strings reach Telegram, public logs, and the user unfiltered. tools_telegram.py has 7 sites using f-string interpolation of raw exception messages (lines 222, 250, 273, 300, 329, 379, 401). pi_agent.py:984 writes god-mode router exceptions to logs/evolution.jsonl via str(e). Raw paths, tracebacks, and potentially API key fragments can leak out.
structural_problem: Every site that surfaces an exception currently does its own (or no) filtering. No single place to enforce redaction rules. New exception sites added in the future inherit the same leakage by default.
target_state: One module exporting safe_error(e, audience). Every outward-facing exception goes through it. Categorical audience flags (user/telegram/public_log/dev) define how much detail is allowed.
design_notes: API: safe_error(e: Exception, *, audience: str = 'user') -> str
audience='public_log' returns category only (type(e).__name__) â€” strictest; for god-mode error logging
audience='telegram' returns redacted single-line, capped at 200 chars
audience='user' returns redacted message, no cap
audience='dev' returns full message with traceback (for internal-only debug paths)
Redaction order: strip tracebacks FIRST (Traceback... block), then paths, then keys
Path regex must catch both Windows (e:\pi\...) and Unix (/Users/...) absolute paths
Key regex covers: sk-* (OpenAI), eyJ* (JWT), AKIA* (AWS), gsk_* (Groq); update as new providers are added
Must handle empty exceptions (Exception()) â€” fall back to type name
Must handle nested causes (e.__cause__, e.__context__) â€” redact each
Must be CHEAP â€” called on every exception in hot paths; no heavyweight parsing
Pure stdlib only â€” no new dependencies
success_criteria: safe_error(Exception('key sk-abc123def456ghi789jkl012mno345pqr678')) does not contain the key substring
safe_error output for audience='telegram' is <= 200 chars
verify.py PASS with new test file
All existing 734 tests still PASS (no behaviour change)
risk_notes: Regex over-redaction could mask useful debug info. Mitigation: audience='dev' bypasses path/key redaction for internal logging; only outward-facing audiences redact heavily.
migration_plan: 1. Create agent/redaction.py with safe_error() + the 3 regex constants (_PATH_RE, _KEY_RE, _TRACE_RE)
2. Add module docstring explaining the 4 audience modes

3. Create testing/test_redaction.py covering: path redaction (Win+Unix), key patterns (sk-/eyJ/AKIA/gsk_), traceback stripping, empty exception, nested cause, telegram length cap, public_log category-only
4. Run pytest testing/test_redaction.py â€” must PASS
5. Run scripts/verify.py â€” must PASS overall
6. No edits to any other file in this ticket â€” wiring happens in T-104
test_plan: test_path_redaction_windows â€” input contains 'e:\pi\.env' â†’ output contains '<path>'
test_path_redaction_unix â€” input contains '/Users/ash/.env' â†’ output contains '<path>'
test_key_redaction_openai â€” input contains 'sk-abc...' â†’ output contains '<key>'
test_key_redaction_anthropic_jwt â€” input contains 'eyJ...' â†’ output contains '<key>'
test_key_redaction_groq â€” input contains 'gsk_...' â†’ output contains '<key>'
test_traceback_stripped â€” input contains 'Traceback (most recent call last):' block â†’ block is removed
test_audience_telegram_length_cap â€” input with 500-char message â†’ output <= 200 chars
test_audience_dev_unredacted â€” paths and keys preserved when audience='dev'
test_nested_cause_redacted â€” Exception with __cause__ containing a path â†’ cause text also redacted
